questions.question:
A priority queue \( Q \) is used to implement a stack that stores characters. PUSH (C) is implemented as INSERT \( (Q, C, K) \) where \( K \) is an appropriate integer key chosen by the implementation. POP is implemented as DELETEMIN\( (Q) \). For a sequence of operations, the keys chosen are in



GATE CSE 1997 | Question: 4.7

Let \( A \) be a priority queue for maintaining a set of elements. Suppose \( A \) is implemented using a max-heap data structure. The operation \( \text{EXTRACT-MAX} (A) \) extracts and deletes the maximum element from \( A \). The operation \( \operatorname{INSERT}(A, key ) \) inserts a new element \( key \) in \( A \). The properties of a max-heap are preserved at the end of each of these operations.

When \( A \) contains \( n \) elements, which one of the following statements about the worst case running time of these two operations is \( \text{TRUE}? \)



GATE CSE 2023 | Question: 36
options: Both \( \text{EXTRACT-MAX} (A) \) and \( \operatorname{INSERT}(A, k e y) \) run in \( O(1) \).
Both \( \text{EXTRACT-MAX} (A) \) and \( \operatorname{INSERT}(A, k e y) \) run in \( O(\log (n)) \).
\( \text{EXTRACT-MAX} (A) \) runs in \( O(1) \) whereas \( \operatorname{INSERT}(A, k e y) \) runs in \( O(n) \).
\( \text{EXTRACT-MAX} (A) \) runs in \( O(1) \) whereas \( \operatorname{INSERT}(A, k e y) \) runs in \( O(\log (n)) \).
questions.correct_answer: Both \( \text{EXTRACT-MAX} (A) \) and \( \operatorname{INSERT}(A, k e y) \) run in \( O(\log (n)) \).
